title: Chapter Ninety-Four: Brilliant River
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The whole world slowed to a crawl.

From across the valley, Azure Qilin Namgung Se-jin watched his sister's headless body collapse. He did not stop it. Could not. Two martial sects counted among the finest in the realm had agreed to death matches.

*So it goes.*

The Family Head stood beside him, cold-blooded and stern as ever. His expression barely hardened. "Young master. Prepare."

"Yes."

One word from his father. Concise as always. A crafty hero who made even his children's lives into cornerstones for the family. Se-jin's whole existence had been a path defined by his father's will.

*Helping Hwa-shin leave home. That was my life's achievement.*

"This unworthy son will go."

The Family Head did not answer. He just slightly nodded his thick neck.

Se-jin walked toward the boy standing with undisturbed breath. Lightning Genius Jeong Yeon-shin stood calmly with two swords at his waist, his blue robe fluttering with a faint bloody fragrance.

*How bold.*

He envied the boy. He had told him before -- about wanting freedom, like the Hwangbo family's Lazy Flame Dragon. Lightning Genius already had it. His warrior's bearing at a young age was dazzling.

They faced each other at ten steps.

"About my sister Mi," Se-jin said. "I won't speak of it. Though lacking, she met an admirable end as a warrior. That's enough."

Yeon-shin said nothing.

"You and I -- one of us, perhaps both, might die. I don't consider this a dispute between Namgung and Desolate Fortress. It's a life-and-death match. If I die, I don't want to close my eyes as a chess piece."

Then the hearty smile returned. "I lived peacefully in Namgung's shadow my whole life. Shackles. Even so, I wanted to break free once. They say there are cases of finding freedom in the gap between life and death. Is it possible with Lightning Genius' capacity?"

Yeon-shin did not answer. He gripped the Desolate Sword.

"Let's begin."

"Yes."

They drew simultaneously.

A dry wind played with their hair. And Yeon-shin felt it -- the invisible forest of swords rising around him. Emperor Sword Form, from the first breath. His body became heavy. Standing was difficult. The air transformed into thousand-jin weight.

*Radiant Wing Step. Can it break through this?*

He changed his mind. Schemes did not suit him. He was a warrior led by instinct.

He stepped. Every muscle strained against the crushing domain. Dust erupted. The load eased.

*Every movement in this fight must be Radiant Wing Step.*

The young master was not surprised. His blade came down.

The collision nearly split Yeon-shin's arm. He layered Fate Defying Scripture across his right side and held. Tremendous vibrations shook the blood vessels in his grip.

*So it's this level.*

From then it was selfless absorption. Two streams of sword paths tangled like chaos. Every exchange was heavy -- heavy swords in a heavy realm. Sparks flew. Radiant Sword Style sprayed hazy light. He used fluid layering to handle the crushing power, and stepped supreme footwork on the spot to compensate for lacking weight.

The young master's blade grazed his cheek. Yeon-shin stepped in. Swords clashed and scraped beside his face with shrieking friction.

*Here.*

He released his left hand. But the young master added force, spinning half a circle, pushing Yeon-shin back while embedding rising movement technique energy. Se-jin maintained distance. Would not approach more than necessary. Whenever Yeon-shin gathered energy for the palm technique, a checking strike came instantly.

"Speaking of freedom," Yeon-shin said, "when you fight like this?"

"Hmm?"

"Being bound by method is stifling."

"Quite good provocation."

Se-jin smirked. His sword bearing changed.

The wide Emperor Sword Form domain compressed into a storm centred on his precious sword. From area suppression to killing technique. Commanding momentum. Ordering the sword. Majesty like an emperor.

The impact was catastrophic. Yeon-shin's grip screamed. The Desolate Sword's blade shuddered as if about to break. He was hurled backward.

*Now.*

He readied his left hand. Blood technique and Buddhist power simultaneously. Se-jin sensed it and charged in a straight line, growing huge before Yeon-shin's eyes. Yeon-shin stepped toward the extending blade. Se-jin tried to protect his sword force -- the trajectory twisted.

That was the instant.

Yeon-shin dug into the gap. Phantom Wing First Step. He passed to the outside. His left hand rose -- blood technique and Buddhist energy surging. Even gathering into technique formation was problematic. It took time.

*Doesn't matter.*

He moved his palm over the young master's blade as it passed, riding Emperor Sword Form's own momentum in reverse. Experience whirled from the centre of his palm, converging to the extreme point, skipping one entire process of activation.

Se-jin spun. Surprisingly swift. His rotating strike met the Desolate Sword. Yeon-shin went in without flinching and extended his hand to the young master's side.

He activated the palm technique.

The world shook.

Clear Night Valley trembled. The explosion created echoes that could not die. Yeon-shin staggered back, recoil screaming through his left hand as if a thunderbolt had detonated inside it.

Then screams -- from the Namgung side.

The dust cleared.

Se-jin's waist was gone. Not even blood yet. A fatal wound. It was a wonder he stood at all.

"Stop!"

The young master's voice. Azure light burned in his eyes as he saw his own family members rushing toward Yeon-shin with bared blades.

"The end of warrior Namgung Se-jin," he said, and his voice carried through the valley like a bell, "I will decide." A long breath. "Do not sully my death."

Silence fell. Complete. Absolute.

Then, slowly, he turned his eyes to his final opponent.

"What is the technique's name?"

Yeon-shin understood. "It has none."

"That's fortunate. I have a name in mind."

"Please speak."

"Shall I call it Brilliant River?" Se-jin's voice was fading now, but steady. "Before my eyes the river of the three paths seems to spread, burning so very brightly. Perhaps because my heart is glad. The character for *brilliant* -- meaning to emit radiance -- seems fitting."

Yeon-shin was still. His chest hurt. Not from the fight.

"It will become martial arts discussed as foremost in the murim," Se-jin said. "That's how it feels. Will you use the name I gave?"

"Eternal Blossom Fist, Brilliant River. I will say so if anyone asks."

"Good."

"You seem to want it widely known. But I must roam the entire murim. My innate nature is such."

Se-jin smiled. Hazy. Distant.

"Yes. Do so. Completely freely..."

At the very end, he did not look at his family members.

He died as one walking the martial path. He took in fully with his eyes the warrior who had defeated him. His breath grew thinner. And scattered without a trace.

Slowly. He stopped breathing with his eyes open.

Azure Qilin -- who had stood firmly planted on the earth -- collapsed.

Messenger birds erupted from every direction, wings beating against the sky. The murim's landscape was overturning. The weight of the young master's death was immeasurable.

Yeon-shin stood as victor. Wind swept through the valley with the festival of energy waves. He gripped his lowered sword, blue robe hem lifting, the golden character for *Desolate* shimmering on his shoulder.

The title *Lightning Genius* was being carved into the Nine Provinces under heaven.

Fame.

---
